The well water's been tasting like copper for a week
Someone's been helping themselves to the storehouse at night
The path to the lower fields is washing out again
The roof on the meeting hall has been leaking since the last storm and no one's fixed it
Rats in the grain cellar, worse than last year by a good margin
Whoever's letting their livestock wander at night needs to pen them properly
The nightwatch has been falling asleep on duty, and everyone knows it
Firewood keeps disappearing from the communal pile faster than it should
The fence along the eastern boundary has been down for a month
The potter and the smith won't stop arguing over the shared workspace
Bread from the last batch of flour tasted off, like something had gotten into it
Someone moved the boundary stones again, same dispute as always
The drainage ditch is clogged and the lower road floods every time it rains
Children playing near the old foundation found loose stones and nearly got hurt
The communal oven hasn't drawn properly since the chimney cracked
A family upstream has been dumping wash water where it flows right past the houses
The bridge planks are rotting through and nobody wants to pay for new timber
Smoke from the charcoal burner drifts into the village when the wind turns
Someone's dog has been killing chickens again and the owner won't take responsibility
The market stalls are getting shabbier every season, and the ground turns to mud
Lantern oil has been running out faster than it should, like someone's skimming the supply
The old couple on the hill road need help with their collapsed wall but they're too proud to ask
Noise from the tavern goes on well past midnight now that the innkeeper stopped caring
The healer's garden got trampled and she's blaming the children, but it looked deliberate
Half the tools in the shared shed have gone dull and nobody maintains them
The footpath between the upper and lower village is overgrown and barely passable
Someone's been burning their refuse instead of hauling it out, and the smell lingers for hours
The granary door doesn't latch properly, and the birds have figured that out
The cobbles near the well are loose and someone's going to turn an ankle
The tanner's runoff keeps seeping into the stream no matter how many times we ask him to fix it
Three of the village lanterns haven't been lit in over a week and nobody's claimed responsibility
The common grazing land is being overused by two families who keep more stock than they should
There's a leak in the storage cellar that's been ruining dried goods all season
The boundary wall near the creek has been crumbling for months and nobody's started repairs
The carpenter charged twice for the same job and won't hear a word about it
Someone keeps leaving the gate open at night, and strays get in
The drinking trough hasn't been cleaned in weeks and the horses won't go near it
The communal woodpile is always short, no matter how much we contribute
The weaver's loom has been clattering at all hours and her neighbors are losing patience
There's a sinkhole forming near the old well that nobody's blocked off properly
The road into the settlement is so rutted that every cart that comes through throws a wheel
The dye vats behind the tanner's shed have been leaking a blue stain across the common ground
Somebody's been grazing their goats on the herb garden plots and pretending otherwise
The shared water pump seizes up every few days and nobody can agree on who should fix it
The stone steps to the upper terrace are cracked and slick after every rain
The baker has been shorting the loaves again and charging the same price
The windmill's been groaning something terrible and the miller says it's fine
A fallen tree has been blocking the back path for two weeks and nobody's moved it
The thatch on the eastern row of houses is thinning and won't last another storm
Someone keeps tying their mule to the signpost and the post is about to snap
The chicken coop near the square smells worse than it should: someone isn't cleaning it
The communal cistern has a crack, and we're losing water faster than we can haul it
The merchant who comes through every month has been selling spoiled salt, and nobody's challenged him
The children's play area is full of nails and broken wood from the last construction project
There are hornets nesting in the eaves of the storehouse and nobody wants to deal with them
The old midden pit is full and nobody's dug a new one
Somebody keeps hanging their washing across the main path where carts need to pass
The root cellar smells like something died in it, and nobody will go check
Two neighbors have been feuding over a property line for six months with no resolution
The settlement bell is cracked and sounds more like a cooking pot than an alarm
The fish drying rack near the road attracts every stray cat in the region
The mortar between the foundation stones of the meeting hall is turning to powder
Whoever was supposed to oil the gate hinges hasn't done it, and the squealing wakes people at dawn
The rendering pit is upwind of the houses again because nobody made the renderer move it
A section of the perimeter fence has been patched so many times it's more rope than wood
The common garden has been overtaken by a weed that nobody can identify or kill
The cistern cover warped in the heat and now debris gets into the water every time the wind blows
The meeting hall benches are splintering and somebody got a sliver the size of a nail last week
There's a persistent damp in the lower houses that nobody can trace the source of
The night soil collector has been irregular and the outhouses are getting unbearable
A section of the road collapsed after the last rain and has been blocked off with a rope and a prayer
The wheelwright won't take jobs unless you bring your own iron, and nobody has spare iron
The candle maker has been cutting his wicks too short and the candles sputter and go out
Foxes have been getting into the root cellars and nobody can figure out how
The shared grinding stone is chipped so badly that half the settlement's flour has grit in it
Somebody dumped ash from their forge into the rain barrel and didn't tell anyone
The log pile behind the hall is stacked so badly it's going to collapse on someone
The rope bridge to the mill platform is fraying and the miller says he can't afford new rope
There's standing water near the livestock pens that breeds flies all summer
The signpost at the crossroads blew down and nobody put it back, so strangers keep getting lost
The communal herb beds have been raided three times this month and nobody's seen a thing
The bark roof on the supply shed curls up in the heat and lets rain in at night
A family's been keeping pigs in their yard and the stench carries for half the settlement
The main path turns to a stream every time it rains because the grading is wrong
The watchtower steps are rotting from the inside and the guards pretend not to notice
The settlement compost heap hasn't been turned in months and it's gone foul
Someone's chickens roost on the meeting hall roof and the droppings are becoming a problem
The clothesline posts near the square have been leaning worse every week
The communal salt supply keeps running low and no one admits to taking extra
The wheelbarrow has a broken handle and we've been sharing the remaining one among thirty people
There's a gap under the storehouse wall where rain gets in and dampens the flour sacks
The hitching post outside the tavern has been chewed through by the mule and leans at an angle
A beehive fell from the old oak and nobody's cleaned up the comb, so the wasps moved in
The latrine trench is too close to the well and someone should have raised that concern years ago
The communal saw is bent and cuts crooked, but nobody wants to pay for a new one
The drying shed leaked all winter and ruined a season's worth of preserved fruit
The stone wall along the north side is shedding rocks onto the footpath below
Somebody repaired the gate with green wood and now it's warped shut
The blacksmith's bellows have been wheezing, and his repairs take twice as long
There's a family of raccoons in the meetinghouse attic that nobody's been able to evict
The lamp at the crossroads hasn't been lit properly in weeks and people keep tripping in the dark
The roof beams of the old storage barn are bowing under the weight of last year's thatch
The well bucket's rope is so frayed that drawing water has become a two-person job
Someone's been using the shared woodworking tools and not putting them back
The settlement dogs have been digging up the burial garden
The ferry rope across the creek snapped last week and nobody's replaced it
There are mushrooms growing inside the grain store, which tells you how damp it is
The public notice board fell off its post and has been leaning against a wall for a month
Someone's cooking fire keeps sending sparks onto the neighboring thatch roof
The communal fishing nets have more holes than net at this point
The stepping stones across the creek have shifted and two of them are submerged now
The goat pen's been raided by something with sharp teeth and nobody's tracked it
The village records got damp because someone left the archive door open during a storm
Whoever shingled the south wall of the meeting hall used green shingles, and they've already curled
The potter's kiln smoke drifts into the schoolroom every morning when the wind is wrong
The shared ladder has a broken rung halfway up and nobody's marked it
There's a puddle in the market square that's been there so long people have named it
The courier's horse broke the fence rail last week and the courier rode off without a word
The communal thread supply has been stretched thin because someone's been taking more than their share
The foundation of the old mill is tilting and the door won't close properly anymore
Someone left tanning hides out in the rain and the runoff has stained the common ground brown
The tavern's chimney is leaning and the innkeeper insists it's always been that way
A cart full of stone was abandoned on the main path three days ago and nobody's moved it
The chicken wire on the coop has rusted through and half the flock is loose
The public well's winch mechanism is grinding so badly it takes twice the effort to draw water
The herb drying racks collapsed in the wind and nobody's rebuilt them
The old footbridge sways so much now that people walk the long way around instead
The shared mending kit has been missing its largest needle for weeks and nobody's owned up
There's a draft in the meeting hall so strong it blows out candles during evening sessions
The compost from the tannery was spread too close to the houses and the stink is relentless
The lumber pile behind the workshop has termites and the carpenter says it's fine
The only barrel of good vinegar went sour because someone left the lid off
The stone bench outside the meeting hall cracked clean in half and nobody's replaced it
Somebody's been taking eggs from the communal henhouse before dawn and denying it
The water channel to the garden plots has silted up and the downstream plots get nothing
The settlement scarecrow fell over a month ago and the crows have been having a festival
The lime wash on the south-facing walls has peeled so badly the whole row looks abandoned
There's a loose shutter on the storage building that bangs in the wind all night
The shared oxcart lost a wheel last season and nobody's had the iron to repair it
The fire pit stones have cracked from overuse and nobody's replaced them
A tree root has pushed up through the floor of the corner house and the family just works around it
The public washing stones are so worn they barely scrub anything clean
Somebody's been stacking their cordwood against the meeting hall wall, and now there's rot
The iron pump handle snapped off in someone's hand and we've been using buckets since
The drainage grate under the road is so clogged that a lake forms every time it drizzles
The settlement's only good axe has been missing for a week and everyone is blaming everyone else
The old elder tree in the square dropped a branch that crushed a market stall and nobody's cleaned it up
There's mold on the ceiling of the meeting hall that nobody wants to acknowledge
The communal smokehouse door won't seal and the last batch of fish was barely cured
Half the fence posts around the livestock pen are leaning at angles that suggest 'fence' is generous
The public bread oven has a crack in the floor that makes every other loaf taste like ash
Somebody's geese have claimed the main path and hiss at anyone who tries to pass
The rainwater barrel at the end of the lane has gone green and nobody wants to empty it
The night lantern outside the meeting hall burns through oil twice as fast as it should
A pile of broken pottery has been sitting behind the kiln for so long it's become a landmark
The communal rope has been shortened by so many people cutting pieces off that it barely reaches the well
There's a hole in the road that fills with water and looks shallow but isn't, and someone falls in weekly
The hinge on the storehouse door screams so loudly you can hear it from the other end of the settlement
Someone keeps moving the rain gauge and nobody can get an accurate reading
The flue in the communal kitchen is blocked and every meal cooked in there tastes of soot
The shared clothesline has snapped so often we've just started draping things over fences
There's a persistent leak in the tavern ceiling that the innkeeper has placed a bucket under as a permanent solution
The settlement flag is so tattered it looks more like a rag, and the flagpole is leaning
Somebody's been leaving fish guts behind the market stalls and the flies are unbearable
The shared hay storage is infested with something that makes the horses refuse it
The path to the privy is unlit and someone twisted their knee last week
The community seed stores have weevils in them, which we discovered at planting time
The public mortar and pestle cracked and we've been passing around a single spare among twelve households
The rain gutter on the meeting hall dumps water directly onto the front steps, making them a sheet of ice in winter
There's a wasp nest in the outhouse that everyone complains about and nobody removes
The bridge toll box has been pried open twice this season and the funds are gone
The communal scales are off-balance and every transaction is an argument
The thatch binder has raised his rates and now half the roofs in the settlement need patching
A cat had kittens in the grain store and now there are cats everywhere you look
The public noticeboard's ink has run so badly from rain that you can't read a single posting
The settlement bell has been rung for everything from dinner to lost goats and it's lost all meaning
Somebody painted their house a color that clashes with everything around it and they seem proud of it
The manure pile behind the stables has grown so large it's visible from the road and it's the first thing visitors see
The communal broom has been missing for a week and people are sweeping with bundles of sticks
The water channel overflow runs right past the baker's door and he's been complaining for three months with no result
The ferry crossing takes twice as long because the ferryman insists on finishing his lunch before making the return trip
Someone left a dead cat in the rain barrel and nobody found it for four days
The public footpath crosses private land now because the landowner moved his fence and everyone's afraid to say anything
There's a rooster in the settlement that crows at every hour of the night and its owner claims it's protecting the community
The road outside the settlement has a rut so deep it swallowed a child's boot and we had to dig it out
The shared grazing land is down to mud and thistles because nobody enforces the stocking limits
The council meetings run so long that half the attendees leave before any decisions are made
Someone's been using the communal washing stone to sharpen tools and now it's got grooves in it
The wheelwright's apprentice damaged three wheels in a row and the wheelwright still lets him take jobs
The night soil man changed his schedule and nobody was informed, so the buckets sat for an extra day
A dead tree has been leaning over the path for two seasons and everyone walks around it like it's scenery
The shared well has a crack in the capstone that nobody's repaired because they can't agree on who should pay
The tanner moved his drying racks closer to the houses and claims the wind has changed direction permanently
There's a puddle at the bottom of the meeting hall steps that freezes into a sheet of ice every winter and every winter someone falls
The settlement's only set of decent scales belongs to someone who charges a fee to use them
Somebody's been tossing scraps over the wall and the stray dogs gather there every morning and fight
The communal butter churn has been left dirty so many times that the last three batches have tasted sour
The path between the houses is so narrow that two people can't pass without one stepping into the mud
The chimney sweep hasn't come through in two seasons and every flue in the settlement is a fire hazard
Someone grafted a branch onto the communal apple tree that produces something sour and inedible
The drainage from the upper houses flows through the lower gardens and nobody upstream acknowledges it
The communal hay fork has been sharpened so many times the tines are needle-thin and useless for actual hay
A section of the road was supposedly repaired last month but whoever did it used sand instead of gravel and it's already gone
The tavern's sign fell down and has been propped against the wall for so long people give directions by the fallen sign
The market weights were supposed to be inspected last season and nobody showed up to do it
The communal firepit smokes no matter which way the wind blows because whoever built it didn't understand drafts
Someone built a wall that blocks the view from the guard post and claims it's within their property rights
The shared tool chest has no lock since the lock rusted through and now things disappear weekly
The threshing floor has a dip in the center that collects water and nobody's filled it because they can't agree on the material
There are nettles growing along both sides of the back path and every child who passes through comes home welted
The grain measure used at market is visibly dented and reads differently depending on which side you look at
Somebody's donkey broke free and ate the flowers the elder planted along the meeting hall and nobody owned up
The bridge railing is missing a section and the solution so far has been a piece of rope and a handwritten warning
The communal soap has been replaced with something that doesn't lather and nobody knows who supplied it
There's an abandoned cart in the field that's been there so long birds have nested in it, and now nobody can move it without disturbing the birds
The settlement's flag rope is tangled at the top of the pole and whoever hung the flag didn't secure it properly
The footpath stones are uneven enough that the elder tripped twice last month and blamed the youth
Someone keeps blocking the drainage grate with straw and the culprit changes their story each time they're asked
The communal oven door won't close properly because someone used it to dry their boots and warped the iron
The well winch squeaks so badly that drawing water at dawn wakes up half the settlement
A pile of construction rubble has been sitting in the square since the building project was abandoned months ago
The shared leather stitching kit is missing its awl and everyone claims they returned it
The bench outside the meeting hall has lost a leg and has been propped on a stone so badly that it tips when anyone sits on the end
There's a damp spot on the meeting hall wall that grows every season and the council's official position is that it's cosmetic
The communal flour sieve has a tear in the mesh that lets half the bran through and nobody's mended it
Someone keeps moving the boundary marker between two gardens at night and both owners insist the other is doing it
The settlement dogs have formed a pack and spend their evenings barking at nothing in particular
The old mill path is so overgrown that anyone using it arrives covered in burrs and scratches
The public drinking cup at the well has been missing for a month and people are cupping water in their hands
The shared grinding stone wobbles on its mount and every user has to wedge something under it before starting
The gutter over the market entrance dumps water directly onto anyone standing below when it rains
The communal thread supply is hopelessly tangled because someone wound three colors onto the same spindle
The night watch lantern hasn't had its glass cleaned in so long that the light barely reaches the ground
There's a family of starlings in the meeting hall rafters and their droppings are becoming a feature of public assemblies
The settlement's shared hatchet has been resharpened so many times the blade is half its original size
The back gate latch is installed upside down and everyone has learned to open it wrong rather than fix it
Someone keeps leaving the barn door open and the draft puts out the forge fire across the way
The hitching rail outside the meeting hall was kicked apart by an ox and has been held together with twine for a season
The oil in the public lamp burns a foul-smelling smoke because someone has been topping it up with rendering grease
There are loose tiles on the meeting hall roof that slide off in strong wind and nobody repairs them between storms
The community seed box was left open during a rainstorm and half the spring planting stock has been lost to mold
The footpath to the well passes through a patch of ground that turns to knee-deep mud in any weather short of drought
The fish trap in the stream catches more debris than fish and nobody wants to clean it because the last person cut their hand
The settlement clock is forty minutes slow and the clockkeeper says it's everyone else who's fast
The privy seats have splinters and the carpenter says they're low on his priority list
The shared cart has a wheel that pulls left no matter how you load it and every trip to the field is a wrestling match
Someone built their woodpile so close to the main path that it's become a single-file passage
The shared rain cloak has been borrowed so many times it smells like every person in the settlement
The fence gate to the common field opens inward instead of outward, which means the livestock push it open and the people can't
The settlement's only mirror cracked and now three families are fighting over who gets to keep the largest piece
